title: helianthemum variabile var. angustifolium
intro: Helianthemum variabile var. angustifolium, also known as rockrose, is a flowering plant in the Cistaceae family. It is a perennial shrub that is native to Europe and Western Asia, valued for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Rockrose can be prepared as a tea, salve, or infusion, and its extracts have been shown to have potential health benefits, including antioxidant and antiseptic properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
